summary: Move automatic project-thumbnail readback out of the viewer render frame after exact hosted AC-15.a measured 111.8 ms while all other Ubuntu browser and M4 journey checks passed.

externalInputs.purpose: Inspect the exact 782e92f Ubuntu browser failure. The unchanged production-static gate measured AC-15.a at 111.800 ms; its other static test, all 24 aggregate browser tests, and the full hosted M4 journey passed.

decisions.decision: Schedule non-user-requested project-thumbnail capture after a 250 ms quiet window rather than starting WebGL-to-2D readback and PNG encoding inside the viewer's rendered-frame callback. Coalesce scheduled frames to the latest identity, retain the existing in-flight newest-result handoff, cancel pending work on unmount, and leave explicit user/MCP screenshot capture immediate and unchanged.
decisions.rationale: The automatic thumbnail became a full live-canvas readback after the memory repair. Starting that persistence-only work synchronously from onFrameRendered can occupy the same main-thread/frame window as the immediate cache interaction measured by AC-15.a. Thumbnail persistence is not visible interaction feedback and can safely yield for a bounded quiet window; cached status, user screenshots, and render semantics must not yield to it.

testEvidence.criterion: Automatic 3D thumbnails must not start in the render-frame callback, must run after the bounded quiet window, must still persist the newest project/document/render identity across overlap, and must cancel scheduled work on unmount.
testEvidence.test: Exercise the production ViewerPane/ModelViewer thumbnail connector with deferred capture promises and real timers.

red.outcome: RED: 2 of 4 integration tests failed in 3.34 seconds. A second frame did not restart the quiet window, and an identity change without a rendered frame let the scheduled old frame invoke capture under the new identity. An earlier RED run also proved the pre-change implementation captured synchronously inside the frame callback.

green.outcome: GREEN: all 4 integration tests passed in 4.18 seconds. Capture remained absent until 250 ms after the latest frame, an in-flight old capture could not bypass the next identity's quiet window, an identity change without a new frame dropped scheduled old work, cross-destination records persisted correctly, and unmount prevented scheduled capture.

nearMisses.sought: Increasing the 100 ms limit, removing either post-mutation animation frame, adding retries, selecting a faster sample, or weakening the status mutation oracle
nearMisses.confirmation: Rejected. The exact one-sample, zero-retry, under-100-ms hosted gate remains unchanged.

nearMisses.sought: Delaying explicit viewport screenshots or returning stale pixels
nearMisses.confirmation: Rejected. Only automatic project-thumbnail scheduling changed; user and MCP captures remain immediate and render the current scene before encoding.

questions: No owner decision is required. Exact hosted AC-15.a confirmation is required on the next committed head.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
